title: Service entrypoint contract
implementation_summary: Added the control-plane Permission And Audit Binding service entrypoint contract for RP00.P02.M05.S01.

The S01 contract, context registry, service module, getter, validator assertion, synthetic fixture, contract definition, README entry, RP00 validator checks, and service tests bind a deterministic metadata-only entrypoint after RP00.P02.M04.S14.

The contract requires the RP00.P02.M04.S14 Claude review required-routing closeout to be production_ready and records five upstream evidence refs for policy, fixture, construction inspection, command evidence, and adjudication.

The entrypoint declares role-check, deny-rule, security-trimming, audit-event, tenant-isolation, Matter-trace, human-approval-boundary, and blocked-claim contexts without executing runtime permission or audit behavior.

The allowed scope remains metadata-only and does not execute service logic, create runtime routes, evaluate runtime permissions, call AuthZ evaluators, run permission engines, apply security trimming, append audit ledgers, write audit events, write database, write storage, persist product state, mutate entity registries, or replace human approval.
The dedicated service tests prove contract binding, no-runtime permission/audit contexts, module/getter/validator behavior, expanded validator drift rejection, and synthetic fixture parity.
Claude review returned PASS_WITH_FINDINGS with no P0/P1/P2 findings. The single P3 unit-level negative-coverage finding was fixed by expanding drift tests before production_ready.
RP00.P02.M05 remains open and hands off to RP00.P02.M05.S02 Request normalization. RP00.P02 and RP00 remain open.
